no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly prescribes the exact fix: use PaddedPODArray<UInt8> for pad_string, use insertFromItself for expansion, use chassert after validation, use validateFunctionArguments with FunctionArgumentDescriptors, use explicit num_chars == 0, use digit separators 1'000'000, use size() not length(), remove specific error codes, add specific headers. solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert gold-specific implementation details: exact string 'PaddedPODArray<UInt8> pad_string' in private section, exact method call 'pad_string.insert(pad_string_.begin(), pad_string_.end())', 'insertFromItself', 'chassert(column_pad_const)', exact function signature 'const ColumnsWithTypeAndName & arguments'. tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Every single test reads the source file and checks for string patterns (e.g., 'if "PaddedPODArray<UInt8> pad_string" not in content'). No test compiles the code, runs the functions, or checks actual runtime behavior. All tests are pure text/grep checks on source code.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub that simply writes the expected strings into padString.cpp (without any functional implementation) would pass all tests, since every test only checks for string presence in the file content. No compilation or execution is performed.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All 11 requirements from instruction.md have corresponding tests: PaddedPODArray usage, empty pad string handling, validateFunctionArguments, chassert, explicit comparison, digit separators, size() method, includes, Allman brace style, error code removal, insertFromItself.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with descriptive function names, docstrings explaining the behavioral rationale, and clear grouping into fail-to-pass and pass-to-pass categories.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB outputs.
